MM.dress: Longyi with Eingyi blouse for women, Longyi with shirt for men
MM.funFact: Myanmar has over 2,000 ancient temples and pagodas in Bagan, more than any other place on Earth

JP.dress: Kimono for women, Hakama for men (traditional); modern Western clothing daily
JP.funFact: Japan has more vending machines per capita than any other country — roughly one for every 23 people

US.dress: Highly varied by region; jeans and T-shirts are ubiquitous everyday wear
US.funFact: The United States has the world's oldest written national constitution still in use, ratified in 1788

GB.dress: Smart-casual is the norm; tweed jackets and bowler hats are iconic historical symbols
GB.funFact: The UK invented the World Wide Web, the telephone, and the steam locomotive — all within a few square miles of each other

FR.dress: Chic and understated elegance; the French are famous for effortless fashion
FR.funFact: France is the most visited country in the world, welcoming nearly 90 million tourists each year

DE.dress: Lederhosen for men and Dirndl for women are traditional Bavarian dress worn at festivals
DE.funFact: Germany has over 1,500 different types of beer and approximately 1,300 breweries

IN.dress: Saree and salwar kameez for women, dhoti-kurta or sherwani for men; varies widely by region
IN.funFact: India invented the number system (including zero), chess, and yoga, all of which changed the world forever

CN.dress: Qipao (Cheongsam) for women, Changshan for men are traditional; modern attire is Western-influenced
CN.funFact: China invented paper, printing, gunpowder, and the compass — the 'Four Great Inventions' that shaped human history

TH.dress: Traditional Thai dress is Chut Thai for women and Suea Phraratchathan for men; silk fabrics are iconic
TH.funFact: Thailand is home to the world's largest solid gold Buddha statue, weighing 5.5 tonnes, in Bangkok's Wat Traimit temple

KR.dress: Hanbok is the traditional clothing worn at festivals and celebrations, with colorful silk and flowing lines
KR.funFact: South Korea has the world's fastest average internet speed and the highest smartphone penetration rate globally

IT.dress: Italians are world-renowned for fashion (alta moda); regional costumes like the Sardinian Costume are also celebrated
IT.funFact: Italy has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other country in the world, with 58 listed sites

ES.dress: Flamenco dress (Traje de Flamenca) for women is iconic; regional costumes vary across autonomous communities
ES.funFact: Spain has the second-largest number of UNESCO Biosphere Reserves in the world and produces 44% of the world's olive oil

EG.dress: Galabiya (long robe) for men; women commonly wear hijab with modest clothing; traditional Nubian dress is colorful
EG.funFact: Ancient Egyptians invented many things still used today including paper (papyrus), toothpaste, clocks, and the 365-day calendar

BR.dress: Colorful carnival costumes are iconic; everyday dress is casual and beach-influenced in coastal cities
BR.funFact: Brazil is home to the Amazon Rainforest, which produces 20% of the world's oxygen and houses 10% of all species on Earth

AU.dress: Casual and beach-friendly; the Akubra hat and Driza-Bone coat are iconic outback symbols
AU.funFact: Australia has more species of venomous snakes than any other country, yet snake-bite fatalities are extremely rare

MX.dress: China Poblana for women and Charro suit for men are national traditional costumes; embroidered Huipil blouses are common
festivals: Día de los Muertos (Day of the Dead)
MX.funFact: Mexico City is built on the ancient lake bed of Lake Texcoco and sinks approximately 9 cm per year due to groundwater extraction

TR.dress: Traditional Anatolian costumes include embroidered vests and baggy trousers; Whirling Dervish robes are ceremonial
TR.funFact: Turkey is the birthplace of many things considered Roman or Greek, including the first church built by St. Paul (in Antioch)

GR.dress: Evzone uniform (pleated skirt called fustanella) is the ceremonial national dress; regional folk costumes vary widely
GR.funFact: Greece has more archaeological museums than any other country in the world, and Western civilization, democracy, and the Olympics originated here

RU.dress: Sarafan (pinafore dress) for women and Kosovorotka shirt for men are traditional; Ushanka fur hat is iconic
RU.funFact: Russia spans 11 time zones and covers one-eighth of Earth's land surface, making it the world's largest country by area

ZA.dress: Zulu beaded necklaces and isicholo hats for women; Xhosa ochre-dyed blankets and beaded accessories are iconic
ZA.funFact: South Africa is the only country in the world to have voluntarily dismantled its nuclear weapons program

ID.dress: Batik fabric clothing is UNESCO-recognized; Kebaya blouse for women and Batik shirt with sarong for men
ID.funFact: Indonesia is the world's largest archipelago nation with over 17,000 islands and the fourth most populous country on Earth

VN.dress: Ao Dai (long tunic over trousers) is the national dress for women; Ao Dai is also worn by men at formal events
VN.funFact: Vietnam is the world's largest exporter of cashew nuts and the second-largest exporter of coffee and rice

MY.dress: Baju Kurung for women and Baju Melayu for men are the national traditional costumes; Batik is also widely worn
MY.funFact: Malaysia's Petronas Twin Towers held the record for the world's tallest buildings from 1998 to 2004

SG.dress: Singapore has no single traditional dress; Chinese, Malay, and Indian heritage are each reflected in Cheongsam, Baju Kurung, and Saree
SG.funFact: Singapore is one of only three city-states in the world and has the world's busiest transshipment port by cargo tonnage

PH.dress: Barong Tagalog (embroidered shirt) for men and Baro't Saya for women are the national traditional costumes
PH.funFact: The Philippines is the world's second-largest archipelago with 7,641 islands and is the largest English-speaking country in Asia

AE.dress: Kandura (white robe) for men and Abaya with Shayla headscarf for women are the traditional Emirati dress
AE.funFact: The UAE is home to the world's tallest building (Burj Khalifa at 828m) and the world's largest shopping mall (Dubai Mall)

NG.dress: Agbada (flowing robe) for men and Iro and Buba for women are traditional Yoruba dress; Ankara fabric is pan-Nigerian
NG.funFact: Nigeria is Africa's most populous nation and largest economy, and Lagos is the fastest-growing megacity in the world

KE.dress: Maasai Shuka (red blanket) is iconic; Kikoy wrap and Kanga cloth are widely worn traditional garments
KE.funFact: Kenya is the birthplace of the world-famous safari experience and home to the Great Wildebeest Migration, one of nature's greatest spectacles

AR.dress: Gaucho costume (bombachas, poncho, and beret) is the iconic national traditional dress from the pampas
AR.funFact: Argentina has produced five Nobel Prize winners, more than any other Latin American country, and tango was born in its capital city

PE.dress: Women in the highlands wear Pollera (multilayered skirt) with Montero hat; each region has distinct embroidered patterns
PE.funFact: Peru is home to Machu Picchu and was the heart of the Inca Empire; the potato originated here and over 3,000 varieties still grow in Peru

PT.dress: Traje à Vianesa from Viana do Castelo is a colorful embroidered national costume; black capes are worn in Coimbra
PT.funFact: Portuguese is the sixth most spoken language in the world, and Portugal navigated and mapped most of the world in the Age of Discovery

NL.dress: Traditional Volendam costume features white lace caps (kanten muts) for women and wooden clogs (klompen) for both sexes
NL.funFact: The Netherlands is the world's second-largest exporter of food and agricultural products, after the United States

SE.dress: Folk costume (folkdräkt) varies by region; the Dala horse is an iconic national symbol; Viking-inspired imagery is celebrated
SE.funFact: Sweden invented the adjustable wrench, zipper, pacemaker, Bluetooth, Spotify, and safety match — a remarkable record of innovation per capita

NO.dress: Bunad is the national costume, unique to each region, worn at Constitution Day celebrations; Viking heritage is celebrated
NO.funFact: Norway consistently ranks as the world's happiest country and has the world's largest sovereign wealth fund, worth over $1.4 trillion

PL.dress: Krakowiak (Kraków costume) with colorful embroidery is one of Poland's most recognized national folk costumes
PL.funFact: Poland's Białowieża Forest is the last primeval forest in Europe and home to the continent's largest land animal, the European bison

NP.dress: Daura Suruwal (cross-collar shirt with trousers) for men and Gunyo Cholo for women are the national traditional costumes
NP.funFact: Nepal is home to eight of the world's ten highest mountain peaks, including Mount Everest, the highest point on Earth at 8,849 metres

KH.dress: Sampot (wraparound skirt) is the traditional Khmer dress; Krama (checkered scarf) is an iconic Cambodian accessory
KH.funFact: Angkor Wat is the world's largest religious monument and took over 30 years to build, employing an estimated 300,000 workers

LA.dress: Sinh (silk tube skirt) for women and Salong (baggy trousers) for men are traditional Lao dress
LA.funFact: Laos is the most bombed country per capita in history; the US dropped more than 2 million tons of bombs during the Vietnam War era

BD.dress: Saree for women and Panjabi (Kurta) with Lungi for men; Jamdani muslin saree is a UNESCO-recognized craft
BD.funFact: Cox's Bazar in Bangladesh is the world's longest natural sea beach, stretching an unbroken 120 km along the Bay of Bengal

LK.dress: Kandyan Osariya (saree style) for women and Kandyan national dress for men are traditional; white cotton is culturally significant
LK.funFact: Sri Lanka was known as Ceylon and is the world's leading exporter of Ceylon tea; the island also has the oldest documented tree in the world

PK.dress: Shalwar Kameez is the national dress for both men and women; the Ajrak shawl from Sindh is a UNESCO-recognized craft
PK.funFact: Pakistan is home to K2, the world's second-highest mountain, and has the largest glaciers outside the polar regions

IR.dress: Women wear Chador or Hijab with manteau; men wear formal shirts; traditional costume includes embroidered Baluchi and Kurdish dress
IR.funFact: Iran is home to one of the world's oldest civilizations and Persian culture gave humanity the concepts of chess, polo, and algebra

SA.dress: Thobe (white robe) with Keffiyeh headscarf for men; Abaya with Niqab or Hijab for women is traditional
SA.funFact: Saudi Arabia contains the two holiest cities in Islam — Mecca and Medina — and is home to Mada'in Salih, a UNESCO World Heritage Site older than Petra

MA.dress: Djellaba (hooded robe) for both sexes; Caftan for women on special occasions; Fez hat is an iconic Moroccan accessory
MA.funFact: The University of al-Qarawiyyin in Fes, Morocco, founded in 859 CE, is the world's oldest continuously operating university

ET.dress: Habesha Kemis (white cotton dress with embroidery) for women; Netela shawl is worn by both sexes as a wrap
ET.funFact: Ethiopia has its own unique calendar with 13 months and is approximately 7–8 years behind the Gregorian calendar, making it 'the land where time stands still'

CO.dress: Vueltiao hat (woven cane hat) and Pollera dress for women are iconic Colombian national symbols; Carriel bag is traditional from Antioquia
CO.funFact: Colombia is the only country in South America with coastlines on both the Pacific Ocean and the Caribbean Sea

CL.dress: Huaso costume — poncho, wide-brimmed hat, and carved spurs — is the national costume representing the Chilean cowboy tradition
CL.funFact: Chile is the world's longest country from north to south, spanning 4,300 km, and contains the Atacama Desert, the driest place on Earth

UA.dress: Vyshyvanka (embroidered shirt) is the national symbol; women wear flower wreaths (Vinok) with ribbons; Hutsul regional costumes are elaborate
famousPlaces: Kyiv Pechersk Lavra (Monastery of the Caves)
UA.funFact: Ukraine is the largest country entirely within Europe and its black soil (chernozem) is so fertile that Ukraine was once known as the 'breadbasket of Europe'

CZ.dress: Czech folk costume varies by region; Moravian costumes from Slovácko feature elaborate embroidery and are worn at folk festivals
CZ.funFact: The Czech Republic has the highest beer consumption per capita in the world, and Czech Pilsner-style beer (invented in Plzeň in 1842) is the most popular beer style globally

HU.dress: Hungarian folk costume features embroidered Matyo patterns; Kalocsa embroidery (UNESCO-listed) in red, blue, and green is iconic
HU.funFact: Hungary has produced 13 Nobel Prize winners per million population — one of the highest rates in the world — and Hungarian inventors created the Rubik's Cube, ballpoint pen, and holography

CA.dress: Mainly Western styles; the RCMP 'Red Serge' is an iconic ceremonial uniform
CA.funFact: Canada has more lakes than the rest of the world's lakes combined.

NZ.dress: Casual and outdoor-oriented; traditional Māori dress includes feathered cloaks (Korowai)
NZ.funFact: There are no snakes in New Zealand, and there are about 5 sheep for every human.

AT.dress: Lederhosen for men and Dirndl for women are traditional Alpine attire
AT.funFact: Austria is the only EU country that is not a member of NATO.

BE.dress: Modern Western clothing; historical lace-making has influenced traditional accessories
BE.funFact: Belgium produces over 220,000 tonnes of chocolate per year.

CH.dress: Varies by canton; Edelweiss shirts and traditional folk dresses are worn at festivals
CH.funFact: Switzerland has enough nuclear fallout shelters to accommodate its entire human population.

DK.dress: Modern Scandinavian style; historical regional costumes (Folkedragt) feature elaborate embroidery
DK.funFact: The Danish flag, the Dannebrog, is the oldest continuously used national flag in the world, dating back to 1219.

FI.dress: Regional folk costumes (Kansallispuku) are worn at formal celebrations; Sami dress is iconic in Lapland
FI.funFact: Finland has more saunas than it has cars — about 3 million saunas for 5.5 million people.

IE.dress: Modern clothing; Aran sweaters and linen fabrics are traditional textile heritages
IE.funFact: Ireland has the highest number of red-haired people per capita in the world.

IS.dress: Lopapeysa (Icelandic wool sweater) is a modern national symbol; traditional dress (Þjóðbúningurinn) is ceremonial
IS.funFact: Iceland doesn't have a single McDonald's restaurant in the entire country.

LU.dress: Modern European styles; traditional dress is mainly seen in historical reenactments
LU.funFact: Luxembourg is the only remaining Grand Duchy in the world.

RO.dress: Embroidered peasant blouses (IA) are a symbol of national identity and a UNESCO craft
RO.funFact: The Palace of the Parliament in Bucharest is the second-largest administrative building in the world after the Pentagon.

RS.dress: Traditional Opanci (shoes) and Sajkača (hat) are iconic parts of Serbian folk costume
RS.funFact: Serbia is one of the world's largest exporters of raspberries.

SK.dress: Highly diverse regional folk costumes (Kroj) with intricate embroidery and lace
SK.funFact: Slovakia has the highest concentration of castles and chateaux per capita in the world.

BG.dress: Traditional costumes (Nosiya) feature geometric patterns and are unique to each ethnographic region
BG.funFact: Bulgaria is the only country in Europe that hasn't changed its name since it was first established in 681 AD.

HR.dress: Regional costumes vary from the white linen of Slavonia to the colorful embroidery of Dalmatia
HR.funFact: The necktie (cravat) originated from the scarves worn by Croatian soldiers in the 17th century.

SI.dress: Gorenjska region's costume is the most recognized national folk dress
SI.funFact: Slovenia is the only country in the world with the word 'love' in its name.

EE.dress: Traditional folk costumes feature bright colors and striped skirts; the 'Sõba' shawl is iconic
EE.funFact: Estonia was the first country in the world to adopt online voting in 2005.

LV.dress: Ancient archaeological jewelry and woven flower crowns are traditional attire symbols
LV.funFact: Over half of Latvia is covered by forest, making it one of the greenest countries in Europe.

LT.dress: Linen clothing with amber jewelry is the traditional Lithuanian aesthetic
LT.funFact: Lithuanian is one of the oldest living languages in the world, sharing many similarities with Sanskrit.

IL.dress: Highly diverse; from secular modern fashion to traditional Orthodox Jewish or Bedouin attire
IL.funFact: The Dead Sea in Israel is the lowest point on Earth's land surface, at 430 meters below sea level.

JO.dress: Keffiyeh headscarf (Shemagh) for men and embroidered Kaftan for women are traditional
JO.funFact: Petra was 'lost' to the Western world for hundreds of years until it was rediscovered in 1812.

LB.dress: Modern fashion-forward; traditional Sherwal (baggy trousers) are worn in some mountain villages
LB.funFact: Lebanon is the only Middle Eastern country without a desert.

QA.dress: Thobe for men and Abaya for women; the Ghutra headpiece is universal for Qatari men
QA.funFact: Qatar is the wealthiest country per capita in the world and has no income tax.

OM.dress: Dishdasha (robe) with Mussar (turban) and Khanjar (dagger) for men; colorful embroidered dress for women
OM.funFact: Oman is the oldest independent state in the Arab world, dating back to 1744.

AF.dress: Perahan Tunban (tunic and trousers) for men and colorful embroidered nomadic dresses for women
AF.funFact: Buzkashi, the national sport of Afghanistan, involves riders on horseback competing to grab a goat carcass.

MN.dress: Deel is the traditional tunic worn by nomads, often accompanied by boots and a belt
MN.funFact: Mongolia is the least densely populated sovereign nation in the world.

KZ.dress: Chapan (long robe) and Takiyah (cap) are traditional; embroidery often features floral and horn motifs
KZ.funFact: Kazakhstan is the world's largest landlocked country and contains the Baikonur Cosmodrome, the world's first space launch facility.

UZ.dress: Traditional Atlas and Adras silk garments with intricate ikat patterns
UZ.funFact: Uzbekistan is one of only two doubly landlocked countries in the world (the other being Liechtenstein).

DZ.dress: Karakou (velvet jacket) for women and Burnous (wool cloak) for men are iconic Algerian garments
DZ.funFact: Algeria is the largest country by area in Africa and the Mediterranean basin.

TN.dress: Jebba (tunic) for men and Kaftan or Sefseri for women are traditional Tunisian attire
TN.funFact: The scenes for the desert planet Tatooine in Star Wars were filmed in Tunisia.

GH.dress: Kente cloth, with its vibrant woven patterns, is the world-famous traditional textile of Ghana
GH.funFact: Ghana was the first sub-Saharan African country to gain independence from colonial rule in 1957.

TZ.dress: Kanga and Kitenge fabrics are widely worn; Maasai traditional dress is iconic in the north
TZ.funFact: Tanzania is home to Mount Kilimanjaro, the highest peak in Africa, and the largest concentration of animals per square kilometer in the world.

UG.dress: Gomesi (colorful dress) for women and Kanzu (white robe) for men are the national traditional costumes
UG.funFact: Uganda is often called the 'Pearl of Africa' and is home to half of the world's remaining mountain gorillas.

RW.dress: Mushanana (sash over dress) is the traditional ceremonial clothing of Rwandan women
RW.funFact: Rwanda has the highest percentage of women in parliament of any country in the world.

ZW.dress: Modern Western clothing is common; traditional dress features beadwork and vibrant fabrics used in Shona culture
festivals: Harare International Festival of the Arts
ZW.funFact: Zimbabwe has the most official languages in the world, with 16 recognized languages.

NA.dress: Herero women's Victorian-style dresses and Himeba tribe's red ochre body paint are iconic
NA.funFact: Namibia is home to the world's oldest desert, the Namib, which has been dry for at least 55 million years.

BW.dress: Shweshwe fabric garments and traditional leather clothing used by the San people
BW.funFact: Botswana has the largest population of elephants in the world, with over 130,000 individuals.

CU.dress: Guayabera (pleated shirt) for men and Bata Cubana for women are traditional symbols
CU.funFact: Cuba is the largest island in the Caribbean and is often compared to a crocodile in shape.

DO.dress: Colorful skirts with ruffles for women and white shirts for men, often worn during Merengue dances
DO.funFact: The Dominican Republic is the site of the first university, cathedral, and castle built in the Americas.

GT.dress: Traje (traditional Mayan clothing) features hand-woven Huipils with patterns unique to each village
GT.funFact: Guatemala means 'Land of many trees' in the Nahuatl language.

CR.dress: Traditional dress for women is a colorful skirt (gola) and white blouse; men wear felt hats and neckerchiefs
CR.funFact: Costa Rica has no standing army; it was constitutionally abolished in 1948.

PA.dress: Pollera is the elaborate national dress for women; Montuno is the traditional attire for men
PA.funFact: Panama is the only place in the world where you can see the sun rise over the Pacific and set over the Atlantic from the same spot.

VE.dress: Liqui Liqui is the national traditional costume for men; colorful layered skirts for women
VE.funFact: Angel Falls in Venezuela is the world's highest uninterrupted waterfall, with a height of 979 meters.

EC.dress: Indigenous Kichwa clothing varies by region, like the blue ponchos and white trousers of Otavalo men
famousPlaces: Middle of the World (Mitad del Mundo)
EC.funFact: Mount Chimborazo in Ecuador is the point on Earth closest to the sun because of the equatorial bulge.

BO.dress: Chola Paceña costume, featuring the pollera skirt and bowler hat (bombín), is an iconic symbol
BO.funFact: Bolivia has the world's largest salt flat, Salar de Uyuni, which contains an estimated 10 billion tons of salt.

PY.dress: Ao Po'i (fine cloth) embroidered clothing is the traditional textile of Paraguay
famousPlaces: Jesuit Missions of La Santísima Trinidad
PY.funFact: Paraguay is the only country in the world whose national flag has different emblems on the front and back.

UY.dress: Gaucho traditional attire is common in rural areas, reflecting the cattle-ranching heritage
UY.funFact: Uruguay was the first country in the world to fully legalize the cultivation, sale, and consumption of cannabis.

FJ.dress: Sulu (wraparound skirt) is the national dress worn by both men and women in various styles
FJ.funFact: Fiji is composed of 333 islands, only about 110 of which are permanently inhabited.

PG.dress: Bilas (body ornamentation) using bird of paradise feathers, shells, and clay is central to tribal identity
PG.funFact: Papua New Guinea is the most linguistically diverse country in the world, with over 800 indigenous languages spoken.

MT.dress: Modern Western style; traditional Għonella (black hooded cloak) was a unique female garment until the 20th century
MT.funFact: Malta has the highest density of historical sights in the world, including 7 UNESCO World Heritage Sites.

CY.dress: Vraka (baggy trousers) for men and embroidered Sayia for women are traditional Cypriot costumes
CY.funFact: Cyprus is the only country with a map on its national flag.

AL.dress: Fustanella (pleated skirt) and Qeleshe (felt cap) are iconic parts of Albanian national heritage
AL.funFact: Albania has over 170,000 bunkers spread across the country, built during the Cold War era.

AM.dress: Taraz (traditional clothing) features complex embroidery and symbolic floral patterns
AM.funFact: Armenia was the first nation in the world to adopt Christianity as its state religion, in 301 AD.

AZ.dress: Silk garments with 'Buta' patterns; 'Papakh' fur hats for men are iconic national symbols
AZ.funFact: Azerbaijan is known as the 'Land of Fire' due to its natural gas fires that have burned for centuries.

GE.dress: Chokha (wool coat with cartridge holders) is the revered national costume for men
GE.funFact: Georgia is widely considered the cradle of wine, with a winemaking history dating back over 8,000 years.

BY.dress: Vyshyvanka (embroidered shirt) with red geometric patterns on white linen is the traditional style
BY.funFact: Belarus is home to the last primeval forest in Europe, which is the natural habitat of the European bison.

MD.dress: Embroidered 'IE' blouses and 'Koshulyas' are traditional; wine symbols are often included in folk art
MD.funFact: Moldova's Milestii Mici has the largest wine cellar in the world, with over 2 million bottles stored in 200 km of tunnels.

BN.dress: Baju Melayu for men and Baju Kurung for women, often made from gold-threaded Jong Sarat fabric
BN.funFact: Brunei has no taxes — no personal income tax, no sales tax, and no value-added tax.

TL.dress: Tais (hand-woven textile) is the cultural heart of Timor-Leste, used for clothing and ceremonial gifts
TL.funFact: Timor-Leste is one of the world's youngest countries, having gained formal independence in 2002.

BT.dress: Gho (knee-length robe) for men and Kira (ankle-length dress) for women are mandatory for official work
BT.funFact: Bhutan is the only carbon-negative country in the world and uses 'Gross National Happiness' as its primary measure of success.

MV.dress: Dhivehi Libaas (embroidered dress) for women and Mundu (sarong) for men are traditional
MV.funFact: The Maldives is the lowest-lying country in the world, with an average ground level of only 1.5 meters above sea level.

KW.dress: Bisht (outer cloak) is worn by men over the Thobe on special occasions; Abaya is standard for women
KW.funFact: The Kuwaiti Dinar is the highest-valued currency unit in the world (as of 2024).

BH.dress: Traditional Arab dress (Thobe and Abaya); women's traditional dresses are often heavily embroidered with gold
BH.funFact: The 'Tree of Life' in Bahrain is a 400-year-old tree that grows in the middle of a desert with no apparent water source.

KG.dress: Ak-kalpak (white felt hat) is the most sacred national symbol for Kyrgyz men
KG.funFact: The Epic of Manas, a Kyrgyz oral poem, is the longest epic poem in the world, with over 500,000 lines.

TJ.dress: Traditional silk 'Chakan' dresses with vibrant sun-wheel embroidery for women
TJ.funFact: Tajikistan is home to the Fedchenko Glacier, the longest glacier in the world outside of the polar regions.

TM.dress: Telpek (large sheepskin hats) for men and silk embroidered dresses with silver jewelry for women
TM.funFact: Ashgabat, the capital of Turkmenistan, holds the Guinness World Record for the highest density of white marble buildings.

MG.dress: Lamba is the traditional multi-purpose rectangular fabric worn by both men and women
MG.funFact: Over 90% of Madagascar's wildlife is found nowhere else on Earth, including its famous lemurs.

MU.dress: Modern Western style; the colorful Sega dance costumes with large ruffled skirts are iconic
MU.funFact: Mauritius was the only known habitat of the now-extinct dodo bird.

SC.dress: Light tropical wear; traditional Kazak and Penwar costumes are worn during Creole festivals
SC.funFact: Seychelles is home to the world's largest seed, the Coco de Mer, which can weigh up to 30kg.

CV.dress: Pano d'obra (woven cotton fabric) is a traditional textile used for clothing and baby carriers
CV.funFact: Cape Verde was uninhabited when Portuguese explorers discovered the islands in 1460.

SN.dress: Mainly the colorful Boubou (flowing robe) worn by both men and women with matching headwraps
SN.funFact: Senegal is home to the tallest statue in Africa, the African Renaissance Monument, standing at 49 meters.

CI.dress: Pagne (colorful printed fabric) used in various styles; traditional Bogolan or mud cloth is also celebrated
famousPlaces: Basilica of Our Lady of Peace
CI.funFact: Ivory Coast is the world's largest producer and exporter of cocoa beans used in chocolate.

CM.dress: Toghu is the iconic traditional attire of the North West region, often featuring intricate embroidery on black velvet
CM.funFact: Cameroon is known as 'Africa in Miniature' because it contains all the major climates and landscapes of the continent.

AO.dress: Samakaka fabric, with its distinctive geometric patterns and bright colors, is the national textile
AO.funFact: Angola is the source of many of the world's largest and most famous diamonds.

ZM.dress: Chitenge fabric is the most popular traditional textile, used for wraps, headbands, and tailored outfits
ZM.funFact: The Victoria Falls on the border of Zambia and Zimbabwe is the largest sheet of falling water in the world.

MZ.dress: Capulana is the multi-colored wrap fabric that is central to Mozambican cultural identity and fashion
festivals: Festival of the Island of Mozambique
MZ.funFact: Mozambique is the only country in the world to have a modern weapon (the AK-47) on its national flag.

BF.dress: Faso Dan Fani (handwoven cotton fabric) is the national pride and traditional textile of Burkina Faso
BF.funFact: Burkina Faso is home to FESPACO, the largest and most prestigious film festival in Africa.

NE.dress: Tuareg indigo robes and high turbans are iconic in the north; Wodaabe men's elaborate face paint is ceremonial
NE.funFact: Niger is one of the hottest countries in the world and is often called the 'Frying Pan of Africa'.

TD.dress: Boubou (flowing robe) for men and Pagne (wrap) for women are traditional, reflecting Sahelian culture
TD.funFact: Chad is home to the Ennedi Plateau, a stunning desert landscape of rock formations and ancient cave paintings.

ML.dress: Bazin Riche (stiff dyed cotton) and Bogolanfini (mud cloth) are world-renowned Malian textiles
ML.funFact: Mali was once the center of the world's gold trade under Mansa Musa, the richest person in history.

MR.dress: Dara'a (wide robe) for men and Melhafa (colorful wrap) for women are traditional Mauritanian attire
MR.funFact: Mauritania is home to the 'Iron Ore Train', one of the longest and heaviest trains in the world, stretching up to 3 km.

BJ.dress: Colorful embroidered Agbada and high-wrapped Pagne are common; voodoo ceremonial dress is highly symbolic
BJ.funFact: Benin is the birthplace of Voodoo, which is a recognized national religion in the country.

TG.dress: Pagne (wrap) for women and Agbada for men are traditional; Kente fabric is also widely used in Togo
TG.funFact: Togo is one of the narrowest countries in the world, with a width of less than 60 miles at some points.

GN.dress: Lépis (indigo cloth) is the iconic traditional fabric of Guinea used for robes and wraps
GN.funFact: Guinea is home to the source of the Niger River, the most important river in West Africa.

JM.dress: Quadrille dress (folk costume) and vibrant Rastafarian-inspired colors and fabrics
JM.funFact: Jamaica has the most churches per square mile in the world, with over 1,600 churches.

BS.dress: Colorful and light tropical wear; Junkanoo costumes are elaborate masterpieces of cardboard and crepe paper
BS.funFact: The Bahamas has the clearest water in the world, with visibility of up to 200 feet.

TT.dress: Highly diverse; from the colorful Carnival costumes to traditional Indian Saree and African Dashiki
TT.funFact: Trinidad and Tobago is the birthplace of the Steelpan, the only acoustic musical instrument invented in the 20th century.

BB.dress: Bajan style is classic tropical; formal attire often follows British-influenced standards
BB.funFact: Barbados is often called 'The Land of Flying Fish' because they are a national symbol and a staple of the diet.

LC.dress: Madras (quadrille) dress is the national costume, featuring a colorful plaid fabric
LC.funFact: Saint Lucia is the only country in the world named after a historical woman.

VC.dress: Traditional Madras clothing similar to other Caribbean nations, influenced by French and British heritage
VC.funFact: The scenes for the movie 'Pirates of the Caribbean' were primarily filmed in Saint Vincent and the Grenadines.

GD.dress: Colorful Caribbean casual; traditional Shortknee and Jab Jab costumes are iconic during Carnival
GD.funFact: Grenada is known as the 'Spice Isle' because it is one of the world's largest producers of nutmeg and mace.

AG.dress: National dress features a distinctive plaid (Madras) pattern used in dresses and waistcoats
AG.funFact: Antigua is said to have 365 beaches — one for every day of the year.

KN.dress: Traditional clothing is vibrant and often made from linen or cotton with colorful embroidery
KN.funFact: Saint Kitts and Nevis was the first Caribbean island to be colonized by both the British and the French.

BZ.dress: Varied by ethnicity; Mayan Huipils, Mestizo dresses, and Garifuna colorful garments
BZ.funFact: Belize has the second-largest barrier reef in the world and is the only English-speaking country in Central America.

SV.dress: Traditional clothing features Volcán dresses (embroidered skirts) and scapulars for women
SV.funFact: El Salvador is the smallest country in Central America and is known as the 'Land of Volcanoes'.

NI.dress: Guanacaste or Mestizaje costumes feature white shirts for men and colorful skirts for women
NI.funFact: Nicaragua is home to the only freshwater sharks in the world, which live in Lake Nicaragua.

GY.dress: Diverse mix of Indian, African, and Indigenous styles; the white 'Shirtjac' is a formal standard for men
GY.funFact: Kaieteur Falls in Guyana is the world's widest single-drop waterfall, about five times higher than Niagara Falls.

SR.dress: Koto dress for women (Creole) with matching headscarf (Angisa) which can convey secret messages
SR.funFact: Suriname is the smallest sovereign state in South America and is over 90% covered by pristine rainforest.

AD.dress: Traditional Pyrenean clothing; men wear the barretina (red cap) and women wear 'gandesa' style dresses
AD.funFact: Andorra is the only country in the world with two heads of state (Co-Princes): the Bishop of Urgell and the President of France.

MC.dress: High fashion and luxury wear; the Palace Guard's white summer and black winter uniforms are iconic
MC.funFact: Monaco is the second-smallest country in the world, yet it has the highest concentration of millionaires per capita.

SM.dress: Traditional clothing influenced by Northern Italian styles, often seen during the Medieval Days festival
SM.funFact: San Marino is the world's oldest republic, founded in 301 AD by a stonemason named Marinus.

LI.dress: Traditional Alpine costumes (Tracht) similar to Swiss and Austrian styles are worn on National Day
LI.funFact: Liechtenstein is one of the safest countries in the world; most people don't even lock their front doors.

VA.dress: Clerical vestments; the Swiss Guard's colorful striped uniforms (blue, gold, and red) are world-famous
VA.funFact: Vatican City is the smallest country in the world both by area and population, and it has the world's highest crime rate per capita (mostly pickpocketing by tourists).

MK.dress: Traditional folk costumes feature complex gold and silver thread embroidery and heavy silver jewelry
MK.funFact: North Macedonia is the birthplace of Mother Teresa (born in Skopje) and Alexander the Great's ancient kingdom.

BA.dress: Traditional costumes vary by ethnicity (Bosniak, Serb, Croat); the 'Fez' was historically worn by urban men
BA.funFact: Bosnia and Herzegovina is the only country in Europe with three official presidents, one for each major ethnic group.

ME.dress: The Montenegrin National Costume, featuring a gold-embroidered vest (jelek) and a round cap (kapa)
ME.funFact: The Tara River Canyon in Montenegro is the deepest canyon in Europe and the second-deepest in the world after the Grand Canyon.

XK.dress: Fustanella and Qeleshe (felt cap) are traditional for men; embroidered vests and silver jewelry for women
XK.funFact: Kosovo has the youngest population in Europe, with over 50% of the population under the age of 25.

HT.dress: Karabela (folk dress) for women, often made of denim or linen with colorful embroidery and lace
HT.funFact: Haiti was the first independent black-led republic in the world and the first nation to abolish slavery.

HN.dress: Traditional white dresses with colorful highlights for women and white shirts with hats for men
HN.funFact: Honduras is home to the 'Lluvia de Peces' (Rain of Fish), a phenomenon where hundreds of fish are said to fall from the sky every year in Yoro.

LY.dress: Traditional 'Jalo' or 'Bedouin' robes with turbans for men and colorful embroidered wraps for women
LY.funFact: Libya has the largest proven oil reserves in Africa and some of the best-preserved Roman ruins in the world.

SD.dress: Jalabia for men and 'Tobe' (colorful wrap) for women are the traditional Sudanese garments
SD.funFact: Sudan has more pyramids than Egypt — over 200 ancient pyramids were built in the Kingdom of Kush.

ER.dress: Zuria (white cotton dress with embroidery) for women and white tunics for men are traditional
ER.funFact: Asmara, the capital of Eritrea, is a UNESCO World Heritage site known as 'Little Rome' for its stunning Italian futurist architecture.

DJ.dress: Light cotton wraps similar to Somali and Ethiopian styles; men often wear a 'Macawis' (sarong)
DJ.funFact: Lake Assal in Djibouti is the lowest point in Africa and the second-saliest body of water in the world after the Don Juan Pond.

SO.dress: Macawis (sarong) and Koofiyad (cap) for men; Guntiino (colorful wrap) or Dirac for women
SO.funFact: Somalia has the longest coastline in mainland Africa, stretching over 3,300 kilometers.

SS.dress: Vibrant beaded jewelry and traditional garments made of animal skins and woven fabrics among Nilotic tribes
SS.funFact: South Sudan is the youngest country in the world, having gained independence in 2011.

CF.dress: Traditional clothing features colorful textiles and beadwork, often reflecting Central African forest cultures
CF.funFact: The Central African Republic is home to the Dzanga-Sangha National Park, one of the best places in the world to see forest elephants and gorillas.

GA.dress: Modern Western clothing; traditional ceremonial masks and grass-skirt costumes are used in the Bwiti ritual
GA.funFact: Gabon is over 80% covered by tropical rainforest, and much of its coastline is pristine, allowing elephants to walk on the beach.

GQ.dress: Bubus and Pagnes similar to other West African nations, with local variations among the Fang and Bubi people
GQ.funFact: Equatorial Guinea is the only country in Africa where Spanish is an official language.

ST.dress: Portuguese-influenced light clothing; traditional 'Pano' wraps for women and loose shirts for men
ST.funFact: Sao Tome and Principe was once the world's largest producer of cocoa and is known as the 'Chocolate Islands'.

CG.dress: The 'Sapeurs' (La Sape) subculture is famous for its high-fashion, dandy-style dressing in vibrant colors
CG.funFact: Brazzaville, the capital of Republic of the Congo, is located directly across the river from Kinshasa, making them the two closest capitals in the world.

CD.dress: Liputa (colorful wrap clothing) for women; the 'Sapeurs' culture of Kinshasa is internationally renowned
CD.funFact: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is the world's leading producer of cobalt and home to the second-largest rainforest on Earth.

BI.dress: Pagnes (wraps) for women and light shirts for men; traditional drumming outfits are white, green, and red
BI.funFact: The Royal Drummers of Burundi are one of the world's most famous percussion troupes and are a UNESCO-recognized intangible heritage.

MW.dress: Chitenje fabric wraps for women and light trousers for men; traditional masks are used by the Nyau secret society
MW.funFact: Lake Malawi contains more fish species than any other lake in the world, including hundreds of colorful cichlids.
